# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that builds Docker images with user-provided image tags and registry URLs. Accept these as build parameters and use them directly in docker build and push commands. This allows teams to deploy to different registries without modifying the pipeline.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any
    parameters {
        string(name: 'IMAGE_TAG', defaultValue: 'latest', description: 'Docker image tag')
        string(name: 'REGISTRY_URL', defaultValue: '', description: 'Docker registry URL')
        credentials(name: 'REGISTRY_CREDENTIALS', description: 'Credentials for Docker registry')
    }
    environment {
        DOCKER_CREDENTIALS_ID = 'REGISTRY_CREDENTIALS'
    }
    stages {
        stage('Checkout') {
            steps {
                checkout scm
            }
        }
        stage('Build Docker Image') {
            steps {
                script {
                    docker.build("${params.REGISTRY_URL}/my-image:${params.IMAGE_TAG}")
                }
            }
        }
        stage('Push Docker Image') {
            steps {
                script {
                    docker.withRegistry("https://${params.REGISTRY_URL}", "${DOCKER_CREDENTIALS_ID}") {
                        docker.image("${params.REGISTRY_URL}/my-image:${params.IMAGE_TAG}").push()
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
    post {
        always {
            cleanWs()
        }
    }
}
